title: Useful Resources
text:
Useful Resources
Some useful resources will be posted here.

title: Beining Liu
text:
Beining Liu







Welcome to my webpage!

I am an incoming PhD student in Economics at New York University Stern School of Business. My research interests lie in economic history, political economy, and applied microeconomics. I am particularly interested in how historical shocks, informal institutions, and state-society interactions shape long-run economic and political outcomes.
Before my PhD studies, I earned an MS in Quantitative Economics at NYU Stern, an MEcon at HKU, and a BEcon at the University of International Business and Economics.
You can find my CV here.
Contact: liubeining [AT] nyu [DOT] edu
SSRN: Beining Liu

title: 刘贝宁
text:
刘贝宁







欢迎来到我的主页！

我将从今年8月起，于纽约大学斯特恩商学院攻读经济学博士学位。我的研究兴趣为经济史、政治经济学与应用微观经济学，特别是非正式制度与文化如何对当今的经济社会现象产生长期影响。

在此之前，我先后获得了对外经济贸易大学的经济学学士学位（国际经济与贸易专业）、香港大学的经济学硕士学位，以及纽约大学的数量经济学硕士学位。此外，我曾于2024-25年于香港大学亚洲环球研究所担任全职研究助理。
这里是我的英文版个人简历。
联系方式： 
liubeining [AT] nyu [DOT] edu

title: Research
text:
Boxer Rebellion Memory and State Response in Modern China


This project studies how local historical memory of the Boxer Rebellion shapes contemporary nationalism, collective action, and government responses in China.




The Great Depression and Entry into Scientific Careers


This project examines whether local exposure to economic distress during the Great Depression affected entry into scientific careers in the United States.







Literacy, Information Diffusion, and the Meiji Restoration in Tokugawa Japan


This project studies whether the spread of terakoya education contributed to information diffusion, political mobilization, and modernization in late Tokugawa Japan.
